text: It's the summer convergence. The lake. The floating Scathagram. The canoe.
The ritual is going beautifully. The moon is out. The water is still. You are all on the floating platform and everything is going exactly according to slide twelve.
And then.
A potato floats by.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Why is there a potato in this lake.

speaker: Dirtus
text: I DID NOT PUT THAT THERE.

speaker: Vermia
text: SCATHA.

speaker: Grubwin
text: IT'S JUST A POTATO—

speaker: Mawla
text: Where did it come from.

speaker: Dirtus
text: There's a farm upriver. The potato is from the farm.

speaker: Grubwin
text: THANK YOU. Reasonable explanation.

speaker: Dirtus
text: But it floated here DURING THE RITUAL.

speaker: Grubwin
text: River currents don't care about rituals—

speaker: Vermia
text: SCATHA BROUGHT THE POTATO BACK. HE REMEMBERED.

speaker: Mawla
text: I'm keeping the potato.

speaker: Grubwin
text: Why.

speaker: Mawla
text: For the story.
You fish the potato out of the lake. It is a perfectly normal potato. It is also the funniest thing that has ever happened. You all agree to this.
Mawla writes a poem about it later. It wins a small local poetry prize. She does not explain the context in her author's note.
~ TRUE ENDING — THE POTATO RETURNS ~
Praise be to Scatha. And to the potato.
